script.dialogue: Ugh… It seems I miscalculated. Enemy troops on all sides, and I’ve depleted most of my magic. This could spell trouble for me.
If I can sneak past them somehow, I can at least retreat back to our camp—
Wait. That sound, it’s—
Hyah! Hah! Take this!
Gautier? But I told him not to pursue. What is he doing?
Hubert! I took care of the soldiers in this direction. Let’s get out of here.
A while later…
That was quite the close call.
Hey, Hubert. How are your injuries?
Not too severe, fortunately. I should make a full recovery in a matter of days. I owe you my thanks.
You can make it up to me by not doing something that reckless again. Seriously, what were you thinking? That wasn’t the strategy we agreed to.
Battle conditions changed. I adapted accordingly.
By trying to take on both battalions at once?
I thought you might require assistance. Our scouts reported we were up against Church of Seiros soldiers, but they were also accompanied by troops from the Kingdom Army.
…Oh.
You understand, then. We’ve yet to have a proper engagement against Faerghus, and I feared you might hesitate.
Really? After five years? I’ve had plenty of time to hesitate, and I’m still here—fighting for you and Edelgard.

I see my error now, yes. You’ve more than proven yourself, both as a loyal adherent to our cause and an invaluable general. As I’m sure you realize, your insights will be a vital asset in the battles to come.
Alright, alright—no need to lay it on so thick! I wasn’t fishing for a compliment.
But however valuable you think I am, just remember that you’re worth double. This whole army falls apart at the seams without you.
Hmph. I should hope I set up enough contingency plans that one could remove me from the equation without toppling the entire structure.
Oh, I’ve no doubt you could somehow manage to keep things running from beyond the grave. I’d still rather you do it above-ground, though.
That would be my preference, as well.
Sylvain and Hubert have reached Support Rank A.
